# LinkedIn Post — Google Launches Gemma 4 Open Model Family — Apache 2.0 Licensed, Built for Agentic Workflows
# Week: Week of 2026-04-06
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting

---

Open-source AI just became a serious enterprise weapon.

On April 3, Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 — its most capable open model family to date. Apache 2.0 licensed. Built on Gemini architecture. Optimized specifically for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows. Runs on-device.

For CTOs evaluating build-vs-buy, this changes the math overnight. A frontier-class open model with agentic capabilities means your team can deploy AI agents that reason, plan, and execute multi-step tasks — without routing sensitive data through third-party APIs. On-device capability means latency drops and data governance stays internal.

The implication is structural: the cost of building proprietary AI workflows just collapsed. Companies that move quickly to fine-tune Gemma 4 for their specific domain will create durable competitive advantages — the same way early cloud adopters did a decade ago. Those who wait for the "next version" will find themselves licensing capability from competitors who already built it.

GYanTech AI helps executive teams translate open-source model releases into deployed business capability — from strategy through production. -> www.gyantechai.com

What is your organization's current timeline from model release to production deployment — and is that fast enough?

## Fact-check notes
- Google DeepMind launched Gemma 4 on April 3, 2026, as its most capable open model family, optimized for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows under Apache 2.0 license (Source: LinkedIn AI Updates March 31 - April 7, 2026 by Dhanushkumar R)
- Gemma 4 is built on Gemini technology with high intelligence-per-parameter and on-device capability, including a 26B-A4B variant (Source: llm-stats.com model tracker, April 2026)
